Uses of Interface
org.docgene.model.XMLParentArticle

  • Packages that use XMLParentArticle 
    Package Description
    org.docgene.api
    This package allows to generate a wiki in another application, using the docGenerator API.
    org.docgene.main
    Contains the entry point of the application.
    org.docgene.model
    Contains the documentation model.
    org.docgene.model.globals
    Contains the global elements for the wiki model.

    • Uses of XMLParentArticle in org.docgene.api

      Methods in org.docgene.api that return XMLParentArticle 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      XMLParentArticle DocGeneratorAPI.getArticle(java.lang.String desc)
      Return the article of a specified description.
      Methods in org.docgene.api with parameters of type XMLParentArticle 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      InternalMenuItem DocGeneratorAPI.addInternalMenuItem(XMLMenu menu, XMLParentArticle article)
      Add an internal menu item to a menu.
      InternalMenuItem DocGeneratorAPI.addInternalMenuItem(XMLMenu menu, XMLParentArticle article, java.lang.String desc)
      Add an internal menu item to a menu.
      XMLReference DocGeneratorAPI.addReference(XMLArticle article, ParagraphElementParent parent, XMLParentArticle refArticle)
      Add a reference.
      XMLReference DocGeneratorAPI.addReference(XMLArticle article, ParagraphElementParent parent, XMLParentArticle refArticle, java.lang.String desc)
      Add a reference.
      XMLReference DocGeneratorAPI.addReference(XMLArticle article, XMLParentArticle refArticle)
      Add a reference at the end of an article.
      XMLReference DocGeneratorAPI.addReference(XMLArticle article, XMLParentArticle refArticle, java.lang.String desc)
      Add a reference at the end of an article.

    • Uses of XMLParentArticle in org.docgene.main

      Methods in org.docgene.main with parameters of type XMLParentArticle 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.lang.String Configuration.getWikiTitle(XMLParentArticle article)
      Return the wiki title, which will be shown in the index page and the articles.

    • Uses of XMLParentArticle in org.docgene.model

      Classes in org.docgene.model that implement XMLParentArticle 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      class  AbstractXMLParentArticle
      An abstract XMLParent article.
      class  OpaqueArticle
      An article which is just an opaque reference.
      class  XMLArticle
      Represents an XML article.
      class  XMLDisambArticle
      Represents an XML disambiguation article.
      class  XMLFallbackArticle
      Represents a fallback article.
      class  XMLIndex
      Represents an XML index article.
      class  XMLRawHTMLArticle
      The element for a raw html article.
      Methods in org.docgene.model that return XMLParentArticle 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      XMLParentArticle Article.getArticle()
      Return the XMLArticle which is really accessed through this Article.
      XMLParentArticle XMLDisambArticle.getArticle()
      Return this.
      XMLParentArticle XMLFallbackArticle.getArticle()
      Return this.
      XMLParentArticle XMLMap.getArticle()
      Return the article in which this Map is used.
      XMLParentArticle XMLMessageBox.getArticle()
      Return the article in which this message box is used.
      XMLParentArticle XMLRedirect.getArticle()
      Return the XMLArticle which is really accessed through this Article.
      XMLParentArticle ArticleReference.getReferencedArticle()
      Return the referenced article.
      XMLParentArticle XMLInclude.getReferencedArticle()
      Return the referenced article.
      XMLParentArticle XMLRedirect.getReferencedArticle()
      Return the XMLArticle which is really accessed through this Article.
      XMLParentArticle XMLReference.getReferencedArticle()
      Return the referenced article.
      XMLParentArticle XMLIndex.getReferToArticle()
      Set the article on which the index is referring to.

      Modifier and Type Method and Description

      Return the articles which include this article.

      Return the articles which include this article.

      Return the articles from which there is a reference to this article.

      Return the articles from which there is a reference to this article.

      Classes in org.docgene.model.globals that implement XMLParentArticle 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      class  ArticlesGlossary
      Maintains a glossary of terms sorted by their first letter.
      Methods in org.docgene.model.globals that return XMLParentArticle 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      XMLParentArticle InternalMenuItem.getArticle()
      Return the referenced article.
      XMLParentArticle ArticlesModel.getArticle(ArticleRef articleRef)
      Return an article specified by its reference.
      XMLParentArticle ArticlesModel.getArticle(java.lang.String refID)
      Return an article specified by its reference.
      XMLParentArticle ArticlesModel.getArticle(java.lang.String refID, java.lang.String pack)
      Return an article specified by its reference.
      XMLParentArticle ArticlesModel.getArticle(java.lang.String refID, java.lang.String origPack, java.lang.String pack)
      Return an article specified by its reference.
      XMLParentArticle ArticlesModel.getFirstArticle(java.lang.String refID)
      Return the first article specified by its reference, regardless of the package in which the article is defined.
      XMLParentArticle ArticlesModel.getFirstArticle(java.lang.String refID, boolean includePackages)
      Return the first article specified by its reference, regardless of the package in which the article is defined.
      XMLParentArticle ArticleCategory.getMainArticle()
      Return the main article of the category.
      XMLParentArticle ArticlesGlossary.Word.getReferencedArticle()
      Return the referenced article.
      XMLParentArticle ArticlesModel.getTransitiveArticle(java.lang.String refID, java.lang.String origPack, java.lang.String pack)
      Return the concrete article for a specified id and package.
